purpose: Define what OpenCompliance publishes publicly versus privately for the first U.S. sector-regime depth wave.

publicBlocked: claiming that all HIPAA Privacy obligations are mechanised or fully satisfied by the current documentary corridor
claiming that a current runbook proves timely and complete notification performance in every real incident

publicAllowed: technical subset mappings for MFA, logging, encryption, vendor oversight, incident response, and disposal
publicBlocked: claiming that the current narrow technical overlap equals a full Safeguards Rule program
claiming that current overlap resolves covered-entity scoping, exemptions, or certification responsibilities

publicAllowed: rights-handling, retention, and service-provider contract mappings
publicBlocked: claiming that the current corridor resolves the whole California privacy-law compliance programme

workflow.name: Choose the narrow overlap worth publishing
workflow.output: A regime-specific control list with explicit proof, attestation, and judgment boundaries

workflow.name: Review public exact anchors
workflow.output: A public exact-anchor layer that cites the authoritative section without importing non-public text

workflow.name: Attach typed evidence contracts
workflow.output: Typed claims and fixture or operator evidence shapes for the mapped controls

workflow.name: Publish only the publication-safe slice
workflow.output: Public status, review, and artifact files that keep remaining gaps explicit
